verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Sophar Naamathites, dixit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֭יַּעַן צֹפַ֥ר הַֽנַּעֲמָתִ֗י וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.king_james: Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid qui multa loquitur, non et audiet ?  aut vir verbosus justificabitur ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲרֹ֣ב דְּ֭בָרִים לֹ֣א יֵעָנֶ֑ה וְאִם־אִ֖ישׁ שְׂפָתַ֣יִם יִצְדָּֽק׃
verses.king_james: Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should a man full of talk be justified?

verses.latin_vulgate: Tibi soli tacebunt homines ?  et cum ceteros irriseris, a nullo confutaberis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּ֭דֶּיךָ מְתִ֣ים יַחֲרִ֑ישׁוּ וַ֝תִּלְעַ֗ג וְאֵ֣ין מַכְלִֽם׃
verses.king_james: Should thy lies make men hold their peace? and when thou mockest, shall no man make thee ashamed?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixisti enim : Purus est sermo meus,  et mundus sum in conspectu tuo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֭תֹּאמֶר זַ֣ךְ לִקְחִ֑י וּ֝בַ֗ר הָיִ֥יתִי בְעֵינֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: For thou hast said, My doctrine is pure, and I am clean in thine eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Atque utinam Deus loqueretur tecum,  et aperiret labia sua tibi,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽאוּלָ֗ם מִֽי־יִתֵּ֣ן אֱל֣וֹהַּ דַּבֵּ֑ר וְיִפְתַּ֖ח שְׂפָתָ֣יו עִמָּֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: But oh that God would speak, and open his lips against thee;

verses.latin_vulgate: ut ostenderet tibi secreta sapientiæ,  et quod multiplex esset lex ejus :  et intelligeres quod multo minora exigaris ab eo  quam meretur iniquitas tua !
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיַגֶּד־לְךָ֨ ׀ תַּ֥עֲלֻמ֣וֹת חׇכְמָה֮ כִּֽי־כִפְלַ֢יִם לְֽת֫וּשִׁיָּ֥ה וְדַ֡ע כִּֽי־יַשֶּׁ֥ה לְךָ֥ אֱ֝ל֗וֹהַּ מֵעֲוֺנֶֽךָ׃
verses.king_james: And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisdom, that they are double to that which is! Know therefore that God exacteth of thee less than thine iniquity deserveth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Forsitan vestigia Dei comprehendes,  et usque ad perfectum Omnipotentem reperies ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַחֵ֣קֶר אֱל֣וֹהַּ תִּמְצָ֑א אִ֤ם עַד־תַּכְלִ֖ית שַׁדַּ֣י תִּמְצָֽא׃
verses.king_james: Canst thou by searching find out God? canst thou find out the Almighty unto perfection?

verses.latin_vulgate: Excelsior cælo est, et quid facies ?  profundior inferno, et unde cognosces ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גׇּבְהֵ֣י שָׁ֭מַיִם מַה־תִּפְעָ֑ל עֲמֻקָּ֥ה מִ֝שְּׁא֗וֹל מַה־תֵּדָֽע׃
verses.king_james: It is as high as heaven; what canst thou do? deeper than hell; what canst thou know?

verses.latin_vulgate: Longior terra mensura ejus,  et latior mari.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲרֻכָּ֣ה מֵאֶ֣רֶץ מִדָּ֑הּ וּ֝רְחָבָ֗ה מִנִּי־יָֽם׃
verses.king_james: The measure thereof is longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si subverterit omnia, vel in unum coarctaverit,  quis contradicet ei ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־יַחֲלֹ֥ף וְיַסְגִּ֑יר וְ֝יַקְהִ֗יל וּמִ֣י יְשִׁיבֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: If he cut off, and shut up, or gather together, then who can hinder him?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse enim novit hominum vanitatem ;  et videns iniquitatem, nonne considerat ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־ה֭וּא יָדַ֣ע מְתֵי־שָׁ֑וְא וַיַּרְא־אָ֝֗וֶן וְלֹ֣א יִתְבּוֹנָֽן׃
verses.king_james: For he knoweth vain men: he seeth wickedness also; will he not then consider it?

verses.latin_vulgate: Vir vanus in superbiam erigitur,  et tamquam pullum onagri se liberum natum putat.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאִ֣ישׁ נָ֭בוּב יִלָּבֵ֑ב וְעַ֥יִר פֶּ֝֗רֶא אָדָ֥ם יִוָּלֵֽד׃
verses.king_james: For vain man would be wise, though man be born like a wild ass’s colt.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu autem firmasti cor tuum,  et expandisti ad eum manus tuas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אַ֭תָּה הֲכִינ֣וֹתָ לִבֶּ֑ךָ וּפָרַשְׂתָּ֖ אֵלָ֣יו כַּפֶּֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: If thou prepare thine heart, and stretch out thine hands toward him;

verses.latin_vulgate: Si iniquitatem quæ est in manu tua abstuleris a te,  et non manserit in tabernaculo tuo injustitia,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־אָ֣וֶן בְּ֭יָדְךָ הַרְחִיקֵ֑הוּ וְאַל־תַּשְׁכֵּ֖ן בְּאֹהָלֶ֣יךָ עַוְלָֽה׃
verses.king_james: If iniquity be in thine hand, put it far away, and let not wickedness dwell in thy tabernacles.

verses.latin_vulgate: tunc levare poteris faciem tuam absque macula ;  et eris stabilis, et non timebis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־אָ֤ז ׀ תִּשָּׂ֣א פָנֶ֣יךָ מִמּ֑וּם וְהָיִ֥יתָ מֻ֝צָ֗ק וְלֹ֣א תִירָֽא׃
verses.king_james: For then shalt thou lift up thy face without spot; yea, thou shalt be stedfast, and shalt not fear:

verses.latin_vulgate: Miseriæ quoque oblivisceris,  et quasi aquarum quæ præterierunt recordaberis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־אַ֭תָּה עָמָ֣ל תִּשְׁכָּ֑ח כְּמַ֖יִם עָבְר֣וּ תִזְכֹּֽר׃
verses.king_james: Because thou shalt forget thy misery, and remember it as waters that pass away:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quasi meridianus fulgor consurget tibi ad vesperam ;  et cum te consumptum putaveris, orieris ut lucifer.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּֽ֭מִצׇּהֳרַיִם יָק֣וּם חָ֑לֶד תָּ֝עֻ֗פָה כַּבֹּ֥קֶר תִּהְיֶֽה׃
verses.king_james: And thine age shall be clearer than the noonday; thou shalt shine forth, thou shalt be as the morning.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et habebis fiduciam, proposita tibi spe :  et defossus securus dormies.
verses.king_james: And thou shalt be secure, because there is hope; yea, thou shalt dig about thee, and thou shalt take thy rest in safety.

verses.latin_vulgate: Requiesces, et non erit qui te exterreat ;  et deprecabuntur faciem tuam plurimi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְֽ֭רָבַצְתָּ וְאֵ֣ין מַחֲרִ֑יד וְחִלּ֖וּ פָנֶ֣יךָ רַבִּֽים׃
verses.king_james: Also thou shalt lie down, and none shall make thee afraid; yea, many shall make suit unto thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oculi autem impiorum deficient,  et effugium peribit ab eis :  et spes illorum abominatio animæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעֵינֵ֥י רְשָׁעִ֗ים תִּ֫כְלֶ֥ינָה וּ֭מָנוֹס אָבַ֣ד מִנְהֶ֑ם וְ֝תִקְוָתָ֗ם מַֽפַּֽח־נָֽפֶשׁ׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: But the eyes of the wicked shall fail, and they shall not escape, and their hope shall be as the giving up of the ghost.
